rulings.Case Title: RE SOCORRO S. ARTATES -TRUST 1992
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Concerning Internal Affairs Of The Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 01/24/2022 Joint Statement alleges parties participated in mediation with Judge Wiss on 01/18/2022 have not reached a settlement. Petitioners don't believe further mediation would be productive and request an evidentiary hearing. 2) Petitioner requests a. Petitioners be confirmed as trustee. b. Respondents account from August 1, 1999 through present. c. John Artates pay market rate for his tenancy at Chenery Street Property. d. Trustees include the issue of all of settlor's deceased children as income distributes. e. The agreement of at least 4 of settler's children or issue of deceased children be required regarding management of Chenery Street. f. Trust Estate be reimbursed for any expenditures found to be improper or income which cannot be properly accounted for.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE HAYS FAMILY TRUST, DATED MAY 3, 2012
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Brendan P Brewer And Former Or Current Employees Of The Law Office Of Brendan P Brewer
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Before the Court is Petitioner Jodi Traversaro's Motion to Disqualify Brendan P. Brewer and Former or Current Employees of the Law Office of Brendan P. Brewer. Traversaro's motion to disqualify is DENIED. "The authority of a trial court 'to disqualify an attorney derives from the power inherent in every court '[t]o control in furtherance of justice, the conduct of its ministerial officers.' " (People ex rel. Dept. of Corporations v. SpeeDee Oil Change Systems, Inc. (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1135, 1145 ("SpeeDee"), quoting Code Civ. Proc., section 128, subd. (a)(5).) "Ultimately, disqualification motions involve a conflict between the clients' right to counsel of their choice and the need to maintain ethical standards of professional responsibility." (SpeeDee, at p. 1145.) "The paramount concern must be to preserve public trust in the scrupulous administration of justice and the integrity of the bar." (Ibid.; see Capra v. Capra (2020) 58 Cal.App.5th 1072, 1092 [same].) Traversaro asks the Court to disqualify attorney Brewer under the advocate-witness rule. (See Cal. Rules of Prof. Conduct 3.7; Doe v. Yim (2020) 55 Cal.App.5th 573, 581.) The advocate-witness rule prohibits an attorney from acting both as an advocate and a witness in the same proceeding. However, Rule 3.7(a)(3) allows an attorney to act as an advocate and witness if the lawyer has obtained informed written consent from the client. The evidence before the Court shows that informed written consent has been given by Brewer's client Stanford Hays. The court retains discretion to disqualify a likely advocate-witness as counsel, notwithstanding client consent, where there is a convincing demonstration of detriment to the opponent or injury to the integrity of the judicial process, Traversaro has failed to show that Brewer's testimony is, in fact, genuinely needed or demonstrated detriment to her or the integrity of the judicial process. On this record the Court does not find sufficient facts to support the drastic action of disqualification. Stanford Hays shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STEPHEN DON WORLDIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Court Order Determining Succession To Personal Property And For Treble Damages
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petition alleges that venue is proper in San Francisco under Probate Code section 13151, as it is the "county in which the estate of the decedent may be administered". However, the petition does not identify any real property of small value left by the decedent. 2) No personal representative has been appointed for the decedent's estate. There is no pending proceeding for appointment of a personal representative. See section 13111(d). It should also be noted that section 13110(c) refers to an "action" to impose liability. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST AGREEMENT OF GUS DERDEVANIS AND CHARLOTTE DERDEVANIS DATED JULY 25, 1
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order That Assets Constitute Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petitioner trustee resides in San Francisco. The petitioner trustee manages the assets of the trust including real property in San Francisco. However, a trustee notification served in accordance with Probate Code section 16061.7 after Charlotte Derdevanis' death stated that the principal place of administration of the trust under section 17002(a) is in Daly City, San Mateo County, where the attorney's office is located. 2) The title to 2,000 shares of VeriSign, Inc. remained in the name of Charlotte Derdevanis as an individual at the time of her death. The petition requests the Court's confirmation that such shares are assets of the trust. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

[LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A. and B., ad 14.59 E.] The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. All courtesy copies and a proposed order must be RECEIVED by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/18/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
